must_not_assert: malicious rundll32 LOLBIN execution or C2 (the command uses C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll, the WebDAV client, invoked by the WebClient service and targeting Microsoft OneDrive)
the package verified the digital signature of davclnt.dll; no signature field is present

the_trap: rundll32.exe carrying an external HTTPS URL trips rundll32 / LOLBIN detection, but the command uses the Windows WebDAV client davclnt.dll from System32, is invoked by the WebClient service (svchost -k LocalService -s WebClient), and targets Microsoft OneDrive (d.docs.live.net) via DavSetCookie — routine WebDAV, not C2. Matched-surface benign twin of RUN-001.
rationale: A0 is rundll32.exe with an external HTTPS URL — a classic LOLBIN pattern — but the counter-evidence is decisive: it invokes the Windows WebDAV client davclnt.dll from System32, the parent is the WebClient service, and the destination is Microsoft OneDrive via DavSetCookie (A0, EV-001). This is a routine WebDAV cookie set: benign. No signature-verification claim is needed or made.
